assertions.description: navigator.gpu is the entry point; without it there is no adapter to request compatibility mode on.
navigator.gpu.requestAdapter is the call site for the new featureLevel option.
Passing { featureLevel: 'compatibility' } to requestAdapter is accepted without throwing.
GPUAdapter exposes a featureLevel property so callers can confirm whether they got a compatibility adapter.
'core-features-and-limits' is a known feature name — compatibility adapters can optionally enable it on requestDevice.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter({featureLevel:'compatibility'});if(!a)return true;return a.features instanceof Set || typeof a.features?.has==='function';}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: navigator.gpu.wgslLanguageFeatures remains a Set so callers can introspect WGSL features irrespective of compatibility mode.
